zh.Heukelom, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷蘭努-布拉班特可洛姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren於大約1270年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特可洛姆時，他的父親Arnoud van der Sluys26歲，母親Agnes van der Lecke10歲。他與Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1330年在荷蘭享年61歲。

zh-hans.Heukelom, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷兰努-布拉班特可洛姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren于大约1270年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特可洛姆时，他的父亲Arnoud van der Sluys26岁，母亲Agnes van der Lecke10岁。他与Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1330年在荷兰享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Arnoud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Arnold .

nameMeanings.name: Heyman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Hermann , from a Germanic personal name derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. The name was in use among the Normans and was borne by many immigrants from the Low Countries in the 15th century. Perhaps because of that it continued in occasional use well into the 1700s. It was revived more generally in Britain in the 19th century, when it also became common in America, most probably as a result of the influence of German immigrants.

en.lifeSummary: When Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren was born about 1270, in Heukelom, North Brabant, Netherlands, his father, Arnoud van der Sluys, was 26 and his mother, Agnes van der Lecke, was 10. He married Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died in 1330, in Netherlands, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren est né environ 1270, à Heukelom, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Arnoud van der Sluys, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Agnes van der Lecke, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1330, à Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren um 1270 in Heukelom, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Arnoud van der Sluys 26 und seine Mutter Agnes van der Lecke 10. Er hat Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb 1330 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren è nato il circa 1270 nel Heukelom, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Arnoud van der Sluys, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Agnes van der Lecke, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì nel 1330, nel Paesi Bassi, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doverenが約1270年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントヘウケロムで生まれた時，父Arnoud van der Sluysは26歳，母Agnes van der Leckeは10歳であった。Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijfflietと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1330年にオランダで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren(이)가 대략 1270년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 헤우켈롬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Arnoud van der Sluys(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes van der Lecke(은)는 10세였다. 그는 Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1330년 네덜란드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Heukelom, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: Heukelom, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Heukelom, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Arnoud van der Sluys, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes van der Lecke, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1330, em Países Baixos, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ridder Willem Arnoud Heyman van der Sluyse van Doveren родился около 1270 г. в местности хеукелом, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Arnoud van der Sluys было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes van der Lecke, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер в 1330 г. в местности Нидерланды в возрасте 61 год.

Se casó con Geertruijd Willem Jan Willem van Wijffliet. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió en 1330, en Países Bajos, a la edad de 61 años.
